title: selinux policy for use with sshd's AuthorizedKeysCommand (podman + gitea)
body:
### Feature request description

Not sure if this is the best place to write here, but I ran into a fairly complex to debug issue. Hopefully if nothing else this issue serves to document it.

I'm trying to set up CoreOS to host gitea.

I followed these instructions: https://docs.gitea.io/en-us/install-with-docker-rootless/#ssh-container-passthrough

This creates a file at 

`/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/70-gitea.conf`

with the following contents 
```
Match User git
  AuthorizedKeysCommandUser git
  AuthorizedKeysCommand /usr/bin/podman exec -i systemd-gitea /usr/local/bin/gitea keys -c /etc/gitea/app.ini -e git -u %u -t %t -k %k
```
My container `systemd-gitea` is being run by the `git` user using quadlet/a user systemd service.

Unfortunately, these instructions don't work with podman due to conflicts with selinux:

```bash
❯ sudo ausearch -c 'sshd' --raw 
type=AVC msg=audit(1679432260.930:359): avc:  denied  { execute } for  pid=2081 comm="sshd" name="podman" dev="vda4" ino=36775453 scontext=system_u:system_r:sshd_t:s0-s0:c0.c1023 tcontext=system_u:object_r:container_runtime_exec_t:s0 tclass=file permissive=0
type=AVC msg=audit(1679432388.316:426): avc:  denied  { execute } for  pid=2305 comm="sshd" name="podman" dev="vda4" ino=36775453 scontext=system_u:system_r:sshd_t:s0-s0:c0.c1023 tcontext=system_u:object_r:container_runtime_exec_t:s0 tclass=file permissive=1
type=AVC msg=audit(1679432388.316:427): avc:  denied  { read open } for  pid=2305 comm="sshd" path="/usr/bin/podman" dev="vda4" ino=36775453 scontext=system_u:system_r:sshd_t:s0-s0:c0.c1023 tcontext=system_u:object_r:container_runtime_exec_t:s0 tclass=file permissive=1
type=AVC msg=audit(1679432388.316:428): avc:  denied  { execute_no_trans } for  pid=2305 comm="sshd" path="/usr/bin/podman" dev="vda4" ino=36775453 scontext=system_u:system_r:sshd_t:s0-s0:c0.c1023 tcontext=system_u:object_r:container_runtime_exec_t:s0 tclass=file permissive=1
type=AVC msg=audit(1679432821.749:329): avc:  denied  { execute } for  pid=2005 comm="sshd" name="podman" dev="vda4" ino=36775453 scontext=system_u:system_r:sshd_t:s0-s0:c0.c1023 tcontext=system_u:object_r:container_runtime_exec_t:s0 tclass=file permissive=0
type=AVC msg=audit(1679432921.520:370): avc:  denied  { read open } for  pid=2119 comm="sshd" path="/usr/bin/podman" dev="vda4" ino=36775453 scontext=system_u:system_r:sshd_t:s0-s0:c0.c1023 tcontext=system_u:object_r:container_runtime_exec_t:s0 tclass=file permissive=0
type=AVC msg=audit(1679432945.018:239): avc:  denied  { read open } for  pid=1374 comm="sshd" path="/usr/bin/podman" dev="vda4" ino=36775453 scontext=system_u:system_r:sshd_t:s0-s0:c0.c1023 tcontext=system_u:object_r:container_runtime_exec_t:s0 tclass=file permissive=0
type=AVC msg=audit(1679433004.809:339): avc:  denied  { read open } for  pid=1831 comm="sshd" path="/usr/bin/podman" dev="vda4" ino=36775453 scontext=system_u:system_r:sshd_t:s0-s0:c0.c1023 tcontext=system_u:object_r:container_runtime_exec_t:s0 tclass=file permissive=0
type=AVC msg=audit(1679433022.644:374): avc:  denied  { read open } for  pid=2034 comm="sshd" path="/usr/bin/podman" dev="vda4" ino=36775453 scontext=system_u:system_r:sshd_t:s0-s0:c0.c1023 tcontext=system_u:object_r:container_runtime_exec_t:s0 tclass=file permissive=0
type=AVC msg=audit(1679433191.667:323): avc:  denied  { execute_no_trans } for  pid=1980 comm="sshd" path="/usr/bin/podman" dev="vda4" ino=36775453 scontext=system_u:system_r:sshd_t:s0-s0:c0.c1023 tcontext=system_u:object_r:container_runtime_exec_t:s0 tclass=file permissive=0
```

Is there a recommendation for an selinux policy for podman? Happy for the issue to be closed but I imagine others might run into this.

For reference , here is `gitea.container`

```systemd
[Unit]
Description=Gitea container
After=local-fs.target systemd-gitea-postgres.service
ConditionPathExists=/var/mnt/data

[Container]
Image=docker.io/gitea/gitea:latest-rootless

# Volume=/var/mnt/data/gitea-authorized-keys:/data/git/.ssh/authorized_keys:z
Volume=/var/mnt/data/gitea-data:/var/lib/gitea:z
Volume=/var/mnt/data/gitea-config:/etc/gitea:z
Volume=/etc/timezone:/etc/timezone:ro
Volume=/etc/localtime:/etc/localtime:ro

# TODO: get rid of published port
PublishPort=0.0.0.0:3000:3000
PublishPort=0.0.0.0:2222:2222

Environment=GITEA__database__DB_TYPE=postgres
Environment=GITEA__database__HOST=systemd-gitea-postgres:5432
Environment=GITEA__database__NAME=gitea
Environment=GITEA__database__USER=gitea
Environment=GITEA__database__PASSWD=REDACTED
Environment=USER=git
Environment=RUN_USER=git

# Map to host user
RemapUsers=keep-id
User=1002
Group=1002

Label=io.containers.autoupdate=registry

PodmanArgs=--privileged

Network=gitea.network

[Service]
Restart=always

[Install]
# Start by default on boot
WantedBy=multi-user.target default.target

# vim: ft=systemd
```

comments.body: One workaround is to set `sshd_t` to permissive by `sudo semanage permissive -a sshd_t`

With the help of some people on matrix (richiedaze) and audit2allow, I wrote this SELinux module:

https://github.com/mjlbach/gitea-selinux/blob/master/gitea-sshd.te

Note, despite there no longer being any messages in `sudo ausearch --success no --message AVC` the policy still does not work, but `setenforce 0` does.

Could you try

container_runtime_domtrans(sshd_t)

And remove your policy.  This will allow sshd_t to directly transition to the podman domain, all of the other rules should no longer be needed.
If this works well, we could add this to container-selinux, perhaps behind a boolean?

Sorry, I'm not familiar that familiar with selinux. Do I need to write something like?

`policy.if`

```
########################################
## <summary>
##	Execute container in the container domain.
## </summary>
## <param name="domain">
## <summary>
##	Domain allowed to transition.
## </summary>
## </param>
#
#
interface(`container_runtime_domtrans',`
	gen_require(`
		type container_runtime_t, sshd_t;
	')

	corecmd_search_bin($1)
	domtrans_pattern($1, container_runtime_exec_t, sshd_t)
')
```
No that interface should be available in container-selinux package.

```
$ rpm -q selinux-policy-devel
selinux-policy-devel-38.8-2.fc38.noarch
$ rpm -q container-selinux
container-selinux-2.205.0-1.fc38.noarch
$ cat mysshd.te 
policy_module(mysshd,0.1)

gen_require(`
        type sshd_t;
')

container_runtime_domtrans(sshd_t)
$ make -f /usr/share/selinux/devel/Makefile 
Compiling targeted mysshd module
Creating targeted mysshd.pp policy package
rm tmp/mysshd.mod.fc tmp/mysshd.mod
$ sudo semodule -i mysshd.pp 
[sudo] password for dwalsh: 
```

Thanks! There is one remaining selinux problem which appears to cause ssh to hang:

```
❯ sudo sealert -a /var/log/audit/audit.log
100% done
found 1 alerts in /var/log/audit/audit.log
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

SELinux is preventing systemd-user-ru from read access on the directory run.

*****  Plugin catchall (100. confidence) suggests   **************************

If you believe that systemd-user-ru should be allowed read access on the run directory by default.
Then you should report this as a bug.
You can generate a local policy module to allow this access.
Do
allow this access for now by executing:
# ausearch -c 'systemd-user-ru' --raw | audit2allow -M my-systemduserru
# semodule -X 300 -i my-systemduserru.pp


Additional Information:
Source Context                system_u:system_r:systemd_logind_t:s0
Target Context                system_u:object_r:iptables_var_run_t:s0
Target Objects                run [ dir ]
Source                        systemd-user-ru
Source Path                   systemd-user-ru
Port                          <Unknown>
Host                          <Unknown>
Source RPM Packages           
Target RPM Packages           
SELinux Policy RPM            selinux-policy-targeted-37.19-1.fc37.noarch
Local Policy RPM              selinux-policy-targeted-37.19-1.fc37.noarch
Selinux Enabled               True
Policy Type                   targeted
Enforcing Mode                Enforcing
Host Name                     nomad
Platform                      Linux nomad 6.1.14-200.fc37.x86_64 #1 SMP
                              PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Sun Feb 26 00:13:26 UTC 2023
                              x86_64 x86_64
Alert Count                   1
First Seen                    2023-03-24 08:48:40 PDT
Last Seen                     2023-03-24 08:48:40 PDT
Local ID                      a7230e92-b7c0-4806-bcf9-47f43035c688

Raw Audit Messages
type=AVC msg=audit(1679672920.602:37024): avc:  denied  { read } for  pid=31193 comm="systemd-user-ru" name="run" dev="tmpfs" ino=115 scontext=system_u:system_r:systemd_logind_t:s0 tcontext=system_u:object_r:iptables_var_run_t:s0 tclass=dir permissive=0


Hash: systemd-user-ru,systemd_logind_t,iptables_var_run_t,dir,read
```
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@mjlbach Have you looked at podmansh, I think that solves this problem nicely. Closing.
